title: CIL and AM Green Aim for India's Largest RE Supply Contract
content: Coal India Limited (CIL) has signed a non-binding Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with AM Green, a platform promoted by the founders of Greenko Group, to supply 4500 MW of renewable energy in phases to AM Greenâ€™s upcoming green ammonia facilities. The renewable power will be sourced through a combination of 2500â€“3000 MW of solar and 1500â€“2000 MW of wind energy, with an estimated investment of â‚¹25,000 crore. Solar projects are planned for high-radiation states like Gujarat and Rajasthan, while wind projects will be explored in southern India. The initiative is part of CILâ€™s strategy to diversify from coal and contribute to Indiaâ€™s clean energy transition and net-zero goals. AM Green will integrate this renewable supply with pumped hydro storage to ensure a reliable, round-the-clock energy flow.
author: Analysts Account
